topology: Op-Amp with Germanium Diode Soft Clipping

components.role: Inverting amplifier — gain stage with germanium diode feedback clipping
Positive-swing germanium clipping diode — very low threshold ~0.3V for early onset clipping
Input resistor — sets gain and input impedance

dsp_model.description: Gain into very soft, low-threshold tanh clipper — germanium diodes have a gradual turn-on curve

behavioral_description: Germanium diodes in the feedback loop create the warmest, most touch-sensitive soft clipping available. The extremely low forward voltage (~0.3V) means clipping begins almost immediately, even with light picking, producing a perpetually warm, slightly compressed tone. The germanium turn-on curve is very gradual compared to silicon, creating an exceptionally smooth transition from clean to clipped. The low output level (max ~0.3Vpp) gives a quiet, intimate character. This is the sound of vintage warmth and sag — the clipping is so soft that it feels more like natural tube compression than electronic distortion. Germanium diodes also have higher leakage current and temperature sensitivity, which contributes to their organic, slightly unpredictable character.
